message:
feat(policy): add PolicyEngine::remove_pane and gc_rate_limiter public forwarders (ft-yjt9e)

Expose two new methods on PolicyEngine that forward to the inner
RateLimiter so long-lived PolicyEngine holders can evict per-pane
rate-limit state when panes disappear:

  pub fn remove_pane(&mut self, pane_id: u64) {
      self.rate_limiter.remove_pane(pane_id);
  }

  pub fn gc_rate_limiter(&mut self) {
      self.rate_limiter.gc();
  }

## Why the forwarders exist

RateLimiter already had the correct internal shape (ft-3l5bu closed
the HashMap-leak bead by adding `remove_pane` + `gc` on the limiter
itself). But ConnectorOutboundBridge and future ObservationRuntime-
side PolicyEngine holders don't see the inner RateLimiter — they
only have access to the engine. Without public forwarders, those
holders would have to either:

  (a) carry a separate reference to the RateLimiter alongside the
      engine (breaks the "one policy engine owns rate-limit state"
      invariant), or
  (b) call engine.rate_limiter.remove_pane(id) directly (requires
      the field to be public, breaking encapsulation).

The forwarders keep the engine's shape honest: rate-limit state is a
concern of the engine, the engine exposes the necessary lifecycle
hooks, and holders only talk to the engine.

## Calling convention

`remove_pane(pane_id)` is intended for PaneDestroyed event handlers
— call it from the same teardown path that already drops other
per-pane state (pane_activity_tracker, BackpressureMetrics::dropped_by_pane,
etc.). Safe to call on a pane_id that was never inserted (no-op
forwards to the limiter's own no-op path).

`gc_rate_limiter()` is defense-in-depth for panes that disappear
without firing a PaneDestroyed event (crashes, abrupt SSH detach,
reaper race). Safe to call periodically from a lifecycle sweep —
O(total timestamps across all tracked panes). Does NOT touch global
rate-limit state — that's already bounded by the finite ActionKind
enum so there's nothing to GC there.

## Two regression tests

1. `rate_limiter_three_panes_destroy_two_leaves_one_entry` (ft-yjt9e
   task-specified shape). Pushes one SendText action for each of 3
   panes, destroys panes 1 and 3, asserts tracked_pane_entry_count()
   == 1 and that pane 2's rate-limit state remains queryable. Uses a
   single ActionKind throughout so the expected post-destroy count
   is unambiguously 1 (not per-action multiplicity).

2. `policy_engine_remove_pane_forwards_to_rate_limiter`. End-to-end
   through PolicyEngine::permissive() to prove the new public
   forwarder actually reaches the inner limiter. Authorizes three
   SendText actions across panes 10, 20, 30 to seed state, then
   calls engine.remove_pane(10) and engine.remove_pane(30) and
   asserts the inner limiter ends up with exactly 1 tracked entry.
   This is the test that exercises the new public API; the first
   is the internal invariant pin.

## Wiring

Plumbing into ObservationRuntime's PaneDestroyed event path is NOT
in this commit — that's a cross-boundary change that belongs on its
own bead. This commit just lands the public surface so the wiring
bead has something to call.

Tracks ft-yjt9e.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
